sections.label: Section 1.2.135
sections.original: Persuasum est homini ; factum est ; ventum est ; vincimur :
sections.translation: He was , persuaded to this . It was carried out ; they came into court : we were beaten . He has married her .

sections.label: Section 1.2.136b
sections.translation: What is it you tell me ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.136c
sections.translation: Just what you have heard .

sections.label: Section 1.2.136d
sections.translation: O Geta , what will become of you ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.137b
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I don ’ t know ; this one thing I do know , whatever fortune may bring , I ’ ll bear it with firmness .

sections.label: Section 1.2.138b
sections.translation: You please me ; well , that is the duty of a man .

sections.label: Section 1.2.139b
sections.original: In me omnis spes mihi est .
sections.translation: All my hope is in myself .

sections.label: Section 1.2.140
sections.translation: I commend you .

sections.label: Section 1.2.140b
sections.original: Ad precatorem adeam credo , qui mihi
sections.translation: Suppose I have recourse to some one to intercede for me , who will plead for me in these terms : Pray , do forgive him this time ; but if after this he does any thing , I make no entreaty : if only he doesn ’ t add , When I ’ ve gone , e ’ en kill him for my part .

sections.label: Section 1.2.144
sections.translation: What of the one who was usher to the Music-girl ? [*]

sections.label: Section 1.2.145b
sections.translation: So so , but poorly .

sections.label: Section 1.2.145c
sections.translation: Perhaps he hasn ’ t much to give .

sections.label: Section 1.2.146b
sections.translation: Why , really , nothing at all , except mere hopes .

sections.label: Section 1.2.147
sections.translation: Is his father come back or not ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.147b
sections.translation: Not yet .

sections.label: Section 1.2.147c
sections.translation: Well , when do you expect your old man ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.148b
sections.translation: I don ’ t know for certain ; but I just now heard that a letter has been brought from him ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.150
sections.original: Et ad portitores esse delatam : hanc petam .
sections.translation: and has been left with the officers of the customs : I ’ m going to fetch it .

sections.label: Section 1.2.151
sections.translation: Is there any thing else that you want with me , Geta ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.151b
sections.translation: Nothing , but that I wish you well . ( Exit DAVUS .) Hark you , boy ( calling at the door ) . Is nobody coming out here ? ( A LAD comes out .) Take this , and give it to Dorcium .

sections.label: Section 1.3.153
sections.original: Adeon rem redisse ut qui mihi consultum optime velit esse ,
sections.translation: That things should have come to such a pass , Phaedria , that I should be in utter dread , of my father , who wishes me so well , whenever his return comes into my thoughts !

sections.label: Section 1.3.155
sections.original: Quod ni fuissem incogitans , ita eum exspectarem ut par fuit .
sections.translation: Had I not been inconsiderate , I might have waited for him , as I ought to have done .

sections.label: Section 1.3.156
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.156b
sections.original: Rogitas , qui tam audacis facinoris mihi conscius sis ?
sections.translation: Do you ask the question ? You , who have been my confederate in so bold an adventure ? How I do wish it had never entered the mind of Phormio to persuade me to this , or to urge me in the heat of my passion to this step , which is the source of my misfortunes . Then I should not have obtained her ; in that case I might have been uneasy for some few days ;

sections.label: Section 1.3.160
sections.original: At non quotidiana cura haec angeret animum .
sections.translation: but still , this perpetual anxiety would not have been tormenting my mind ( touching PHAEDRIA ) .

sections.label: Section 1.3.160b
sections.translation: I hear you .

sections.label: Section 1.3.161
sections.original: Dum exspecto quam mox veniat qui adimat hanc mihi consuetudinem .
sections.translation: While I am every moment expecting his return , who is to sever from me this connection . [*]

sections.label: Section 1.3.162
sections.original: Aliis quia defit quod amant aegre est : tibi quia superest dolet .
sections.translation: Other men feel uneasiness because they can not gain what they love ; you complain because you have too much . You are surfeited with love , Antipho . Why , really , upon my faith , this situation of yours is surely one to be coveted and desired .

sections.label: Section 1.3.165
sections.original: Ita me Di bene ament ut mihi liceat tam diu quod amo frui ;
sections.translation: So may the Gods kindly bless me , could I be at liberty to be so long in possession of the object of my love , I could contentedly die . Do you , then , form a judgment as to the rest , what I am now suffering from this privation , and what pleasure you enjoy from the possession of your desires ; not to mention how , without any expense , you have obtained a well-born and genteel woman , and have got a wife of unblemished reputation :

sections.label: Section 1.3.170
sections.original: Beatus ni unum hoc desit , animus qui modeste istaec ferat .
sections.translation: happy you , were not this one thing wanting , a mind capable of bearing all this with moderation . If you had to deal with that Procurer with whom I have to deal , then you would soon be sensible of it . We are mostly all of us inclined by nature to be dissatisfied with our lot .

sections.label: Section 1.3.173
sections.original: At tu mihi contra nunc videre fortunatus , Phaedria ,
sections.translation: Still , on the other hand , Phaedria , you now seem to me the fortunate man , who still have the liberty , without restraint , of resolving on what pleases you best :

sections.label: Section 1.3.175
sections.original: Retinere , amare , amittere . Ego in eumn incidi infelix locum
sections.translation: whether to keep , to love on , or to give her up . I , unfortunately , have got my-self into that position , that I have neither right [*] to give her up , nor liberty to retain her . But how ’ s this ? Is it our Geta I see running this way ? ’ Tis he himself . Alas ! I ’ m dreadfully afraid what news it is he ’ s now bringing me .

sections.label: Section 1.4.179
sections.original: Nullus es , Geta , nisi iam aliquod tibi consilium celere repperis :
sections.translation: Geta , you are undone , unless you instantly find out some expedient ;

sections.label: Section 1.4.180
sections.original: Ita nunc imparatum subito tanta te impendent mala ,
sections.translation: so suddenly do such mighty evils now threaten me thus unprepared , which I neither know how to shun , nor how to extricate myself therefrom ; for this daring step of : ours can not now any longer be kept a secret . If such a result is not adroitly guarded against , these matters will cause the ruin of myself , or of my master .

sections.label: Section 1.4.183
sections.translation: Why , I wonder , is he coming in such fright ?:

sections.label: Section 1.4.184
sections.original: Tum temporis mihi punctum ad hanc rem est . Herus adest .
sections.translation: Besides , I ’ ve but a moment left for this matter — my master ’ s close at hand .

sections.label: Section 1.4.184b
sections.translation: What mischief is this ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.185
sections.original: Quod quum audierit , quod eius remedium inveniann iracundiae ?
sections.translation: When he comes to hear of it , what remedy shall I discover for his anger Am I to speak ? I shall irritate him : be silent ? I shall provoke him : excuse myself ? I should be washing a brickbat . [*] Alas ! unfortunate me ! While I am trembling for myself , this Antipho distracts my mind . I am concerned for him ; I ’ m in dread for him : ’ tis he that now keeps me here ; for had it not been for him , I should have made due provision for my safety , and have taken vengeance on the old man for his crabbedness ;

sections.label: Section 1.4.190
sections.original: Aliquid convasassem , atque hinc me protinam coniicerm in pedes .
sections.translation: I should have scraped up something , and straightway taken to my heels away from here .

sections.label: Section 1.4.191
sections.original: Quam hic fugam aut furtum parat ?
sections.translation: I wonder What running away or theft it is that he ’ s planning .

sections.label: Section 1.4.192
sections.original: Sed ubi Antiphonem reperiam ? aut qua quaerere insistam via ?
sections.translation: But where shall I find Antipho , or which way go look for him ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.193
sections.translation: He ’ s mentioning your name .

sections.label: Section 1.4.193b
sections.original: Nescio quod magnum hoc nuntio exspecto malum .
sections.translation: I know not what great misfortune I expect to hear from this messenger .

sections.label: Section 1.4.194
sections.translation: Why , are you in your senses ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.194b
sections.original: Domum ire pergam : ibi plurimum est .
sections.translation: I ’ ll make my way homeward ; he ’ s generally there .

sections.label: Section 1.4.195
sections.translation: Let ’ s call the fellow back .

sections.label: Section 1.4.195b
sections.translation: Stop , this instant .

sections.label: Section 1.4.195c
sections.translation: Heyday — with authority enough , whoever you are .

sections.label: Section 1.4.196b
sections.translation: Geta !

sections.label: Section 1.4.196c
sections.translation: The very person I wanted to find .

sections.label: Section 1.4.197
sections.original: Cedo quid portas , obsecro ? atque id , si potes , verbo expedi .
sections.translation: Pray , tell me what news you bring , and dispatch it in one word , if you can .

sections.label: Section 1.4.198
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so .

sections.label: Section 1.4.198b
sections.translation: Out with it .

sections.label: Section 1.4.198c
sections.translation: Just now , at the harbor —

sections.label: Section 1.4.198d
sections.translation: What , my father ?—

sections.label: Section 1.4.198e
sections.translation: You ’ ve hit it .

sections.label: Section 1.4.198f
sections.translation: Ruined outright !

sections.label: Section 1.4.198g
sections.translation: Pshaw !

sections.label: Section 1.4.199
sections.translation: What am I to do ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.199b
sections.translation: What is it you say ?
